Fixed in commit 6b05643, merged to main via d6f218d. Verified live on Foundry v13.351 (dnd5e).

Implemented & verified. Branch feat/embedded-actor-sync: 9 commits, 22/22 unit tests. Live Playwright verification on dnd5e v13 (World A): push (new+existing-entry reconcile), hook-driven debounced push, keepId stable ids incl nested effects, pull reconcile create/update/delete, actor-level effects, transferred-effect safety, shutdown packData fix, next-login durability catch-up — all PASS.

REFUTED on dnd5e v13. getEmbeddedCollection('ActiveEffect') and toObject().effects are symmetric (both exclude transferred effects; transferred effects have parent=Item, surfaced only via actor.appliedEffects). Verified live via Playwright: after pull reconcile of actor-level effects, the ring's transferred effect stays applied and is never marked toDelete.

REFUTED on dnd5e v13. keepId preserves nested ActiveEffect _ids on all create paths. Verified live: deleting ring locally then pulling recreates it with nested effect count=1 and stable _id y9ZyasmFzQ4Ju18E — no churn.
Obsolete: this branch removed SyncEngine.flushPending entirely (it caused the beforeunload->push compendium-write race / packData shutdown error). No flushPending = no N+1 there.
